Description: Main Linux computer / KoalaByte Blue companion brain
Notes: Use stable regulated 5.1 V rail from buck converter; do not feed raw battery voltage

Item: Nordic nRF52840 USB Dongle / PCA10059 / NRF52840-DONGLE
Description: Only retained Nordic BLE board for the dongle-only build
Notes: Plug into Raspberry Pi USB; no separate battery wiring

Description: Dual-eye UI / microphone / display front-end
Notes: Power from regulated 5 V rail and connect to Raspberry Pi by USB serial

Description: 240x240 round LCD eyes
Notes: Included on the ESP32-S3 DualEye module

Description: Audio output for local status / companion feedback
Notes: Mount behind front grille or internal speaker location; use amp module from 5 V rail if required

Description: External Wi-Fi/Bluetooth antenna with fitted lead for ESP32-S3 DualEye
Notes: The Wireless Tracker V2 uses onboard 2.4 GHz antenna hardware unless the exact board revision documents an external 2.4 GHz connector

Description: Small external LoRa antenna for Heltec Wireless Tracker V2
Notes: Match the purchased board band such as 470-510 MHz 863-870 MHz or 902-928 MHz; do not substitute a 2.4 GHz antenna

Item: Heltec Wireless Tracker V2 USB-C LoRa GNSS node board
Description: Optional Didgeridoo LoRa/Mesh/GNSS node
Notes: ESP32-S3FN8 SX1262 LoRa and UC6580 GNSS; connect to Raspberry Pi USB-A with short USB-A to USB-C data cable; do not wire this USB node to Raspberry Pi GPIO

Item: Heltec USB-A to USB-C data cable
Description: Internal USB data cable for Didgeridoo node
Notes: Use a short data-capable cable; charge-only cables will not enumerate as ttyUSB or ttyACM

Description: Mechanical case/top-plate feature for Wireless Tracker V2 LoRa antenna
Notes: Add separate smaller LoRa hole or bulkhead location for the Wireless Tracker V2 LoRa antenna

Description: Mechanical non-metal clearance zone above Wireless Tracker V2 GNSS antenna path
Notes: Keep battery cells speaker magnets metal screws metal standoffs and dense wiring away from the GNSS antenna area

Item: External GNSS antenna and case opening
Description: Optional GNSS antenna hardware if the build switches to GNSS IPEX/U.FL
Notes: Only add this if intentionally using the documented external GNSS antenna path; onboard GNSS antenna path is the default assumption

Item: Adafruit tactile button switch 6mm pack PID 367
Description: Six-button front-panel controls
Notes: Signal wiring only; GPIO to button and other side to GND; do not connect to battery positive or 5 V

Item: KoalaByte Blue front button bezel 6x6mm RevA6
Description: 3D printed front button bezel
Notes: Aligns six front buttons in the enclosure

Description: 2S battery pack cells
Notes: Use matched protected cells from reputable source; inspect wraps and polarity before install

Description: Protected 2S battery holder or holder plus separate 2S BMS
Notes: Use correct series polarity and true 2S protection; holder alone does not replace a BMS unless it includes true 2S protection

Description: Primary battery protection for 2x18650 series pack when not built into holder
Notes: Wire B- B1/BM B+ and P-/P+ exactly; verify before connecting electronics

Description: Main battery positive fuse holder
Notes: Install close to BMS P+; 18-20 AWG leads preferred

Description: Main battery fuse
Notes: Start with 5 A for bring-up; replace only after fault inspection

Description: Main on/off control
Notes: Route after fuse and before buck converter VIN+; use DC current-rated switch and strain relief

Description: Regulated 5 V rail for the Pi and modules
Notes: Feed from 2S battery/BMS through fuse and switch; set output to 5.1 V before connecting electronics; 5 V 5 A preferred if available

Description: Regulated positive power distribution
Notes: WAGO lever connector or screw terminal rail; do not use breadboard rail for main power

Description: Common ground distribution
Notes: All modules share GND after buck converter output

Description: Optional 5 V rail stabilizer
Notes: Install across regulated 5 V rail only; positive to 5 V stripe/negative to GND; use 10 V minimum 16 V preferred

Item: Seloky USB-C PD Trigger Board Module PD/QC Decoy Fast Charge USB Type-C to 12V
Description: Optional external bench/service input path
Notes: Not the primary battery path and not a battery charger; do not tie PD output and battery output together without proper power-path control

Description: Pi OS / logs / reports / captures
Notes: 128 GB recommended; 32 GB minimum for basic testing

Description: Internal power and data harnesses
Notes: Use short data-capable USB cables and label each harness; use heavier wire for main battery and 5 V rail paths

Description: Mechanical stacked assembly
Notes: Use insulating standoffs where clearance is tight

Description: Open-frame stacked module structure
Notes: No custom PCB required

Item: 3D printed case / stacked frame / rear panel
Description: Optional production enclosure with koala-ear form factor
Notes: Print and test-fit before final wiring; leave clearance around batteries fuse switch buck BMS Wireless Tracker V2 LoRa antenna and GNSS sky-view zone

Item: InnoMaker USB to CAN Converter kit
Description: Optional Koala Kan Kommander USB-to-CAN adapter
Notes: Mount internally or in a side/rear service bay; use as SocketCAN interface such as can0 on Linux

Description: Adapter-matched CAN_H / CAN_L / GND / optional SHIELD connection
Notes: No circular panel CAN port; route to authorized bench harness with strain relief

Description: Printed bracket adhesive mount Velcro or standoff support for InnoMaker converter
Notes: Keep clear of antennas battery contacts speaker grille and Raspberry Pi GPIO

Notes: Keep wires away from antennas vents battery contacts BMS fuse optional Wireless Tracker V2 and optional InnoMaker CAN adapter
